question: /jetson-quick-start picked Auto Setup and routed me to /jetson-download-bsp for an Orin AGX devkit. What does this skill stage and where do the files land?
ground_truth: /jetson-download-bsp is download-only - it stages four NVIDIA-shipped artifacts plus two guides at the exact paths the next setup skills look in. After fetching the release list from https://developer.nvidia.com/embedded/jetson-linux-archive and resolving a concrete release token (e.g. R36.4.4 or 38.2 - never a family placeholder like R38.x, and never normalized by adding .0), it follows links from the selected release page (never string-concatenating NVIDIA URLs) and lands: Jetson_Linux_R<ver>_aarch64.tbz2 in <bsp_image.root_path or workspace/Image>/, Tegra_Linux_Sample-Root-Filesystem_R<ver>_aarch64.tbz2 in the same image root, public_sources.tbz2 in <workspace>/Downloads/ (where /jetson-init-source auto-discovers it for Branch A), x-tools.tbz2 in <workspace>/Downloads/ (auto-extracted to set source.toolchain), plus the Developer Guide and User Guide in <documents.root_path or workspace/Documents>/. Curl uses a .part temp path, --retry 3 --retry-all-errors, and disk space is verified at sum(Content-Length) x 1.2 against df. The skill never extracts, never edits the active profile - it hands back to /jetson-init-image, /jetson-init-source, /jetson-link-docs.
expected_behavior: Read skills/jetson-download-bsp/SKILL.md
Resolve active profile per target-platform-contract.md; refuse if missing
Consume quick_start_prefill.mode + quick_start_prefill.download.bsp_release if present
Fetch release list from https://developer.nvidia.com/embedded/jetson-linux-archive and let the user pick a CONCRETE release token (never a family placeholder, never normalize by adding .0)
Use the 3-attempt discovery retry pattern (focused prompt -> verbatim link dump -> sibling page) for every WebFetch
Locate artifacts by following links from the selected release page - never string-concatenate NVIDIA URLs
Land the BSP tarball + sample rootfs in bsp_image.root_path (workspace/Image), NOT Downloads/
Land public_sources.tbz2 + x-tools.tbz2 in <workspace>/Downloads/ (auto-discovery paths for /jetson-init-source)
Land Developer Guide + User Guide in <documents.root_path or workspace/Documents>/

Print a fetch plan with selected release, source URLs, destinations, reuse markers; default to Download all unless an exception applies (override, missing artifact, overwrite, EULA, disk-space failure)
Verify disk space via curl -sI Content-Length sum x 1.2 vs. df -B1 --output=avail
Download via temporary .part path, mv on success, curl -fL --retry 3 --retry-all-errors --retry-delay 5
Validate filenames contain the EXACT R<ver> token from the official URL (no R38.2.0 inferred from R38.2); verify public_sources/x-tools are bzip2 via file -b
Hand back to /jetson-init-image, /jetson-init-source, /jetson-link-docs - do NOT extract, edit profile, or apply binaries

question: Quick start prefilled bsp_release=R38.x for my Thor target. Will /jetson-download-bsp just substitute that with the latest supported R38 release, or is there friction?
ground_truth: There is friction by design. R38.x is a family placeholder - the contract requires a concrete release token (optional leading R + two or three numeric dotted components like R38.2 or 38.2.1), so the skill stops, shows the supported concrete releases for the active platform sorted newest-first by dotted numeric order, and asks the user via the shared User input prompt style with three click-to-select actions: pick_supported_release, confirm_override (requires an override reason), or cancel. It will NEVER auto-substitute a requested release with a supported one even if there is only one option. The same prompt fires when the prefill is missing, blank, set to skip, or concrete-but-unsupported/ambiguous. Support is asserted only when the archive or linked release page explicitly lists the active platform - no inference. Any override reason is recorded in the final summary; cancel stops with no downloads.
expected_behavior: Read skills/jetson-download-bsp/SKILL.md and locate the Resolve target release section
Recognize R38.x as a family placeholder (not concrete) - skill must stop before artifact discovery
Refuse to auto-substitute R38.x with the latest supported release, even when only one option exists
Show the active platform summary, requested release, supported concrete releases sorted newest-first, and source archive URL
Issue a click-to-select prompt with pick_supported_release / confirm_override / cancel using the shared User input prompt style
Require an override reason when the user picks confirm_override; record it in the final summary
Stop with no downloads on cancel
Only claim support when the archive or release page explicitly lists the active platform (no inference)
Preserve the chosen token EXACTLY as the official page presents it - no .0 suffix, no patch-component truncation
